description: Comparing a MAC, token, or other secret with a short-circuiting equality operator, leaking it byte-by-byte via timing.
body: Failure mode: code verifies an HMAC tag or API token using a normal equality check that returns as soon as bytes differ, so response time correlates with how many leading bytes matched, letting an attacker recover the secret across many requests. Detection signals: secrets compared with ==/equals/memcmp that short-circuits; verification time varies with input; no constant-time function in the secret path. Do not compare secrets with early-exit equality. Safer fallback: use a constant-time comparison (crypto.timingSafeEqual, sodium_memcmp, hmac.compare_digest) over fixed-length values, avoid branching on partial matches, and add a test asserting the comparison path does not short-circuit.
